Title: A new class of plant lipid is essential for protection against phosphorus depletion

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: protection against phosphorus depletion stress; maintenance of membrane environment and chloroplast function under phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glucuronosyldiacylglycerol and sulfoquinovosyldiacylglycerol biosynthesis during phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Diacylglycerol; presumably UDP-glucuronic acid for GlcADG synthesis; UDP-sulfoquinovose for SQDG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an anionic sugar headgroup to diacylglycerol in chloroplasts; under phosphate deficiency synthesizes GlcADG, and also catalyzes SQDG formation from UDP-SQ and DAG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for GlcADG accumulation under phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GlcADG accumulation and protects plants from phosphorus depletion; sqd2 mutants lacking both SQDG and GlcADG show severe bleaching and growth defects under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lipid profiling and mutant analysis showed that GlcADG accumulation under phosphate starvation requires SQD2. 2. Independent sqd2 mutants failed to accumulate both GlcADG and SQDG, unlike sqd1 and ugp3 mutants that still accumulated GlcADG. 3. sqd2 mutants were most severely damaged under phosphate depletion, indicating SQD2-derived GlcADG is important for protection against P limitation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis in independent sqd2 alleles; lipidomics by HILIC-MS; comparative phenotyping under phosphate sufficiency and deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coding gene expression is coordinated in green tissues and significantly upregulated by phosphorus limitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Green tissues; induced by phosphorus limitation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted lipidomics by HILIC-MS; Supplementary Data Set.xls
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption causes severe growth defects, bleaching, reduced chlorophyll, and deregulated phospholipid remodeling under phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper proposes SQD2 likely uses UDP-glucuronic acid as sugar donor for GlcADG, but exact donor and pathway remain unconfirmed; possible interconversion with SQDG not excluded
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve tolerance to phosphate-poor environments by sustaining anionic glycolipid remodeling, but engineering should preserve balanced membrane lipid composition

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: parallel pathway component not required for target-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assembles UDP-glucose and sulfite into UDP-sulfoquinovose for SQDG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not required for GlcADG synthesis; sqd1 mutants still accumulate GlcADG and do not show the severe phosphate-starvation phenotype seen in sqd2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sqd1 mutants remained able to accumulate GlcADG under phosphate depletion. 2. This shows SQD1 is not involved in GlcADG synthesis even though it functions in SQDG biosynthesis. 3. Moderate GlcADG accumulation in sqd1 may compensate for SQDG deficiency under phosphate starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant lipid profiling by HILIC-MS; phenotype comparison under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coordinated in green tissues and significantly upregulated by phosphorus limitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Green tissues; induced by phosphorus limitation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted lipidomics by HILIC-MS; Supplementary Data Set.xls
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SQDG deficiency but no severe visible growth defect under tested phosphate starvation when GlcADG is still present
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested pathway gene in Results, but evidence indicates it is not part of the GlcADG biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less direct value for engineering GlcADG accumulation because it is dispensable for GlcADG synthesis

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: parallel pathway component not required for target-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces UDP-glucose used upstream in SQDG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not required for GlcADG synthesis; ugp3 mutants still accumulate slightly higher GlcADG under phosphate depletion and avoid severe sqd2-like damage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ugp3 mutants accumulated GlcADG at slightly higher levels than wild type under phosphate depletion. 2. This demonstrates UGP3 is not required for GlcADG synthesis. 3. Persistence of GlcADG in ugp3 mutants correlates with milder phosphate-starvation phenotype than sqd2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant lipid profiling by HILIC-MS; comparative phenotype analysis under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coordinated in green tissues and significantly upregulated by phosphorus limitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Green tissues; induced by phosphorus limitation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted lipidomics by HILIC-MS; Supplementary Data Set.xls
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SQDG deficiency but no severe visible growth defect under tested phosphate starvation when GlcADG is still present
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested pathway gene in Results, but evidence indicates it is not part of the GlcADG biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value for boosting GlcADG because UGP3 is dispensable for its synthesis
